title: Implement verifyIndexerResult for spot-check verification
markdown:
## Goal

Implement `verifyIndexerResult()` -- a spot-check mechanism that compares indexer output against the source node to detect stale or corrupted index entries. This provides trust-but-verify capability for indexed data.

## Background

Indexers are derived data. They can become stale (missed a write), corrupted (bug in extract function), or tampered with (if the indexer runs on untrusted infrastructure). `verifyIndexerResult()` picks random results from the indexer, reads the original record from the source node, re-runs the extract function, and compares.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `verifyIndexerResult(options)` function:
  - `source: NodeProtocolInterface` -- the source of truth
  - `backend: IndexerBackend` -- the indexer to verify
  - `definition: IndexerDefinition` -- the extraction logic
  - `sampleSize?: number` (default 10)
  - `query?: IndexQuery` -- optional query to scope verification (e.g., verify only recent entries)
- [ ] Returns `VerificationResult`:
  - `verified: number` -- count of entries that matched
  - `mismatched: number` -- count of entries that differed
  - `missing: number` -- count of entries in indexer but not in source
  - `details: Array<{ uri: string; status: "ok" | "mismatch" | "missing"; expected?: Record<string, unknown>; actual?: Record<string, unknown> }>`
- [ ] For each sampled entry: read from source, extract fields, deep compare with indexed fields
- [ ] Handles gracefully: source record deleted (mark as "missing"), extraction returns null (mark as "should_not_be_indexed")
- [ ] Tests: all-matching verification, mismatch detection, missing source detection

## Implementation Guide

1. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/verify.ts`:
   - Query backend with optional scope query, sample `sampleSize` random entries
   - For each entry: `source.read(uri)` -> `definition.extract(uri, data)` -> deep compare with indexed fields
   - Collect results
2. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/verify_test.ts`:
   - Use MemoryClient as source, mock backend
   - Test happy path (all match)
   - Test mismatch (modify indexed data to differ from source)
   - Test missing (delete source record after indexing)
3. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/verify.ts` -- new: verification implementation
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/verify_test.ts` -- new: tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add export

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/verify_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-02 (backend), G1-A6-03 (extraction logic)
- **Blocks**: Nothing
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
